updated_conclusion: The state 0x14 authCode/OTL postwrite callback is an instance of the client-wide runtime callback-manager pattern rooted at 0x140E57B40. The generic release thunk at 0x14002CF10 uses the same selected-entry -> vtable slot +0x20 callback shape and exits through indirect tail jumps. Reverser now models those tail jumps, and the local IDA Free install is captured as an available second-opinion validation tool.

tooling_added.new_capability: Detect IDA Free / IDA Pro from common Windows install paths, not just PATH.
tooling_added.local_result: IDA Free 9.3 detected at C:\Program Files\IDA Free 9.3\ida.exe.
tooling_added.why_this_matters: IDA Free can now be recorded in the repeatable tool inventory and used as a manual graph/xref validation lane without replacing Reverser artifacts.

tooling_added.new_capability: Recover indirect tail jumps such as JMP R10 in addition to indirect CALL sites.
tooling_added.why_this_matters: The generic 0x14002CF10 runtime release thunk tail-dispatches through JMP R10, so call-only recovery missed the callback target even though the decoder had the instruction.

verification: The regenerated 0x14002CF10 dispatch artifact reports two tail-jump dispatches at 0x14002CF5A and 0x14002CF67.
The local IDA inventory artifact reports IDA Free / IDA Pro as available through common-windows-path discovery.

recommended_manual_checks: Open win64c/original/rs2client.exe and jump to 0x14002CF10 to validate the generic runtime callback/release thunk graph.
Jump to 0x14029E970 and 0x14029E9CE to validate the state 0x14 authCode postwrite callback block.
Name 0x140E57B40 as a runtime callback manager candidate and apply temporary field names for +0x100, +0x108, +0x198, +0x1A0, +0x1A8, and +0x1F8.
Export any confirmed labels/xrefs back into Reverser artifacts rather than leaving the knowledge only in an IDA database.
ida_free_status.note: This milestone did not launch the IDA GUI automatically; the detection is recorded and ready for manual validation.

address_ref_scan.interpretation: This is a very broad process/runtime manager, not a state-14-specific object.

known_fields_from_callback_shape.+0x100: Compares against +0x108 to decide whether the active entry is stored in the vector/list or falls back to +0x198.
known_fields_from_callback_shape.+0x108: Current/end-like pointer used with +0x100; when not equal, selected entry is read from [value - 0x8].
known_fields_from_callback_shape.+0x1A0: Compares against +0x1A8 to decide whether count/length comes from [value - 0x4] or fallback +0x1F8.

global_manager.bounded_inference: 0x140E57B40 behaves like a runtime callback/allocator manager that selects an active entry and count from vector-like state before invoking vtable slot +0x20.

generic_release_thunk_0x14002cf10.coverage_note: 0x14002CF10 is not represented as a .pdata runtime function, so explicit ranges are required for Reverser scans.

control_flow.observation: Copies incoming object pointer RCX to R9 and returns immediately if RCX is null.
Selects the callback entry from [RDX+0x108] / [RDX+0x100], using [RAX-0x8] when the range is non-empty or [RDX+0x198] as fallback.
Loads the selected entry vtable/head from [RCX], then reads callback slot +0x20 into R10.
Selects R8D from [RDX+0x1A8] / [RDX+0x1A0], using [RAX-0x4] when non-empty or [RDX+0x1F8] as fallback.
Tail-dispatches to callback slot +0x20 with RDX restored to the incoming object pointer and R8D set to the count/length value.

shared_pattern: Both paths load the same global manager at 0x140E57B40.
Both select a callback entry through +0x100/+0x108 or fallback +0x198.
Both load callback slot +0x20 from the selected entry vtable/head.
Both derive R8D from +0x1A0/+0x1A8 or fallback +0x1F8.
state14_relationship.difference: 0x14002CF10 is a generic null-checked release thunk that tail-jumps through R10 with the incoming object restored in RDX. The state 0x14 path is inline after authCode +0x38 preparation and calls R10 as a manager notification/continuation while keeping the selected entry in RCX.
state14_relationship.updated_bounded_inference: The state 0x14 callback is likely runtime-manager notification or lifecycle dispatch for the prepared authCode/OTL string, not the scheduler edge that invokes the later 0x1401F6D51 consumer.

opennxt_relevance.server_meaning: This maps native runtime/lifecycle machinery around OTL/authCode strings. It does not add a JS5 or game-login packet requirement by itself.
opennxt_relevance.practical_value: For OpenNXT 947 server work, this helps filter native allocator/callback noise away from protocol work. The server-side task remains to supply compatible JS5/login/auth state, while this client-side path explains how the desktop binary prepares, notifies, and later consumes OTL/authCode data.

next_targets: Use IDA Free manually to validate graph branches at 0x14002CF10 and 0x14029E970..0x14029E9CE, then export labels back into artifacts.
Trace callers or thunks into 0x14002CF10 / 0x1407B8478 to classify which object families use the runtime manager release path.
Continue hunting the scheduler edge that invokes 0x1401F6D51 for child state 0x14; the manager callback does not appear to be that direct edge.
Promote the 0x140E57B40 field layout into a reusable tool/worklist if more manager-backed callback paths need clustering.
